what_it_does: Records the presence, intended role, and operational status of the nomic-ai/nomic-embed-code model as a code-specialized embedding substrate, including tentative launch configuration, deployment trade-offs, and open evaluation questions.
observations: Status is downloaded but never test-loaded (runtime: not-yet-served).
It is a ~7B Qwen2-based code-specialized embedding model in FP16, 27 GB on disk, with a native 32,768-token context.
Proposed vLLM launch uses --runner pooling on port 8001 with --trust-remote-code, but the known-good config is explicitly not yet defined.
At inference the model is expected to occupy ~14 GB VRAM, so it cannot co-exist with the chat default and is swap-only or on-demand.
If made the default embedder, bge-m3 would swap out, losing multi-output and prose quality.
Open questions cover vLLM load behavior, embedding dimension, throughput, quality on Tim-relevant code, non-English identifiers/comments, and practical chunk size.
The file notes that prose embedders perform poorly on code, justifying a code-specific embedder for codebase/repo retrieval.
Mixed-content routing (code chunks to this model, prose chunks to BGE-M3) is raised as an unresolved design problem.
standouts: Code-specialized embedding option positioned for codebase/repo retrieval.
Downloaded but unserved, so all performance and deployment notes are provisional.
VRAM footprint forces a swap-only or on-demand deployment pattern rather than a permanent default.
conventions: Uses frontmatter with type, id, filename, status, runtime, date_added, and tags.
Follows a model-card structure: at-a-glance table, source rationale, fits-these-needs link, tested performance checklist, launch config, quirks, open questions, and connects-to backlinks.
Uses [[...]] wiki-style internal links to relate this entry to other ledger files.
concerns: Known-good launch config is explicitly not defined and contains placeholders such as <large; ~14 GB weights at FP16>.
All performance claims are speculative because the model has never been loaded or served.
The disk-to-VRAM estimate is an inference, not a measured value.
No concrete embedding dimension, throughput number, or quality benchmark is recorded.
notes: A placeholder model card for a large code-specific embedder that is downloaded but untested, with deployment constrained by VRAM and mixed-content routing still open.
questions: What is the exact embedding dimension?
Does the model load cleanly via vLLM with --runner pooling?
What is concurrent throughput under expected load?
How does retrieval quality compare on Tim's code corpora versus prose embedders?
What practical chunk size works best for typical source files?
How well does it handle non-English identifiers and comments?
Will deployment be on-demand, swap-only, or run-alongside given the VRAM math?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose (document the Nomic Embed Code model as a code-specialized embedding substrate) matches the content; it is a model card with operational notes and open questions.
apparent_themes: Code-specific embedding outperforms general prose embedders for code retrieval.
Operational pragmatism driven by VRAM and swap constraints.
Experimental substrate inventory with explicit uncertainty.
intention_signals: Track a candidate code embedding model, justify its role over prose embedders, and surface the operational and evaluation work needed before it can be deployed.
novelty: Explicitly frames a 7B code-specialized embedding model as a swap-only or on-demand resource rather than a default, and foregrounds mixed-content routing as an open design problem.
contribution: A record that a code-specific embedding model is available, its approximate resource cost, its intended codebase/repo-retrieval role, and the unresolved operational and evaluation questions that must be answered before deployment.
summary_for_embedding: Model card for nomic-ai/nomic-embed-code, a ~7B FP16 Qwen2-based code-specialized embedding model with a 32768-token context, downloaded but not yet served, intended for codebase/repo retrieval with swap-only or on-demand deployment due to an expected ~14 GB VRAM footprint.
intention_for_embedding: To document and evaluate a code-specific embedding substrate option and the deployment trade-offs involved in swapping it with the general embedder.
